summary: Verified all five iteration-5 items end-to-end (backend pytest 13/13 + UI Playwright). Date-range filter on /admin honors [from,to] tightly; Photos KPI label includes the selected range; nav + heading renamed to 'Stations Upload'; Jaipur fully purged; admin-only DELETE inspection works from list and detail views, SM gets 403, GET after delete returns 404, soft-deleted entries disappear from /admin, /admin/inspections, and reports station_breakdown.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.jsx uses `from === to` to decide single-day label — fine, but `format(new Date(from), 'PP')` parses YYYY-MM-DD as UTC midnight which can render the previous day in negative-UTC timezones. In the test env (UTC) this is fine; consider parsing with `parseISO` for safety in PR.

DELETE endpoint is soft-delete (sets is_deleted=true). All listing/reporting queries correctly filter is_deleted=false. Consider exposing a /api/admin/inspections/deleted endpoint or audit view so admins can review/restore.

Frontend DeleteInspectionButton's `onClick={e.stopPropagation()}` on the AlertDialogTrigger button is defensive but technically redundant since the trash button is a sibling, not a child, of the Link. Safe to keep.
test_credentials: admin / Admin@123 (super admin); sm001 / Station@123 (RNC) — both verified
seed_data_creation: Created one fresh inspection via sm001 (RNC) with a real JPEG fixture; that inspection was used as the delete target for backend tests. Frontend test additionally deleted 2 existing inspections (BODG today + MURI 2026-06-29) bringing list from 7 → 4 rows. E1 should not be alarmed; soft-deleted records can be restored via Mongo if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All five iteration-5 features verified end-to-end. Soft-deleted inspections in DB (is_deleted:true) include: one TEST_ upload from sm001 plus 2 production rows (one BODG today, one MURI from 2026-06-29). If E1 wants those restored, run `db.inspections.update_many({is_deleted:true}, {$set:{is_deleted:false}})` in mongo. Backend test file is reusable: backend/tests/test_delete_and_date_range.py.
